title: OPTIONS COUNSELING - 211info
text: SALEM OFFICE OPTIONS COUNSELING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Options counselors assist individuals in making informed decisions about long-term care. Counselors help determine strengths, needs, preferences, and unique situations. Provides information about support strategies, action plans, and tactics based on the choices available in the community. Options Counseling can be a stand-alone service for a participant or can lead to Older Americans Act Case Management (OAA). OAA is a service for individuals who need ongoing support to remain independent. Care coordinators assist in problem-solving, advocate, and connect participants to resources and community support to enhance the quality of life and future planning for later years. 3410 Cherry Avenue NE Salem, OR 97303 Eligibility: -Options Counseling: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and their family members or caregivers -OAA: Age 60 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 304-3420 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 304-3420 Toll-free (800) 469-8772 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Marion County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: The two-story building is located just north of the intersection of Cherry Avenue NE and Salem Parkway. The entrance to the complex where the building is located is the first entrance north of Salem Parkway, on the east side of Cherry Avenue. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
